title: Triphora lutea
taxonomy: Triphora lutea is a species of sea slug, specifically a type of nudibranch. It belongs to the phylum Mollusca and the class Gastropoda. As a member of the family Triphoridae, it is closely related to other small sea slugs in this family. Triphora lutea is further classified as being in the order Triphorelloidea, with its genus and species name specifically denoting its unique characteristics. This classification helps scientists to understand the evolutionary history and relationships of this species.

intro: Triphora lutea, also known as Madre Selva, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Central and South America, characterized by its climbing habit and small, yellow flowers. Traditionally, Triphora lutea has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues, due to its adaptogenic and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Triphora lutea include coumarins, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal effects.
